title: thelypteris longifolia f. glandulosa
intro: Thelypteris longifolia f. glandulosa, also known as the glandular maiden fern, is a fern species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique glandular hairs and elongated fronds. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which have been attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic content. The health benefits of Thelypteris longifolia f. glandulosa include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antioxidant and anti-aging agent. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used in traditional folk remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
